title: Patient → person + location (multi-table)
notes:
The MULTI-TABLE branch: one Patient resource fans out to BOTH the `person` table (Patient__person.sql) AND the `location` table (Patient__location.sql). The varying input dimension across these variants is the **address** — everything else (gender=male/birthsex M → 8507; US Core OMB race 2054-5 → 8516 Black or African American; OMB ethnicity 2186-5 → 38003564 Not Hispanic or Latino; birthDate 1992-01-25) is held CONSTANT so the only diff is address presence.

The location_id link is the load-bearing cross-table fact. It is NOT hash(Patient.id): both edges compute `stringToId(concat_ws('|', location_line, location_city, location_state, location_zip))` over the patient's own address composite, so `person.location_id` == `location.location_id`. Two patients at the same address would share this key (OMOP CDM v5.4 LOCATION ETL convention: "Each instance of a Location in the source data should be assigned this unique key"). There is no separate FHIR Location resource — the synthetic ref `ref:patient-1-location` denotes this address-derived composite key; the runner resolves it to `stringToId(line|city|state|zip)`, not to a fhir entry.

Stage-1 caveat (Patient__person.view.json): the address subselect uses `forEachOrNull: "(address.where(use='official') | address).first()"`, so a Patient with NO address still emits one staging row with all `location_*` columns NULL. Then:
  - person.location_id = stringToId(concat_ws('|', NULL,NULL,NULL,NULL)) = stringToId('') = NULL (stringToId returns NULL for empty input, see mapspec/etl/_functions.sql).
  - Patient__location.sql has `WHERE location_city/state/zip/line IS NOT NULL` — with all four NULL the row is filtered out, so the location table is EMPTY for that patient (variant b).

Location columns (Patient__location.sql, target column order location_id, address_1, address_2, city, state, zip, county, location_source_value, country_concept_id, country_source_value, latitude, longitude):
  - address_1 ← line.join(' ') (stage-1 location_line). Synthea emits one line entry here.
  - address_2 ← NULL::varchar (hardcoded NULL in the SQL — second line is never populated).
  - city/state/zip ← Address.city/state/postalCode verbatim. Synthea zip keeps the leading-zero '00000'.
  - county ← Address.district — Synthea omits district in its FHIR export, so county is NULL (verified empty in live cdm_ours_fhir.location).
  - location_source_value ← left(concat_ws(', ', line, city, state, zip), 50): the comma-joined composite, capped at 50 chars. For '564 Connelly Esplanade, Chelmsford, MA, 00000' (45 chars) no truncation.
  - country_concept_id ← cm.country_iso_to_omop on country='US' → 4330442 (vocab.concept: 4330442 'United States of America', domain Geography, vocabulary SNOMED, standard 'S'). country_source_value = 'US' verbatim.
  - latitude/longitude ← geolocation extension valueDecimal on the Address (numeric cast).

person columns are identical to the single-table Patient→person happy path (case 01): gender_concept_id 8507 MALE via cm.gender_to_omop on birthsex 'M' (which also carries source_concept_id 8507, the HL7 v3 uppercase code loaded in Athena's Gender vocab); race_concept_id 8516; ethnicity_concept_id 38003564; race/ethnicity source_concept_id 0 (OMB codes are not loaded as concept_code under any Athena race/ethnicity vocabulary, per CLAUDE.md). provider_id and care_site_id are NULL (no generalPractitioner / managingOrganization).

cases.desc: Patient WITH a full home address → fans out to person + location; person.location_id == location.location_id (address-composite hash)
Patient with NO address → person row only (location_id NULL); location table EMPTY (Patient__location WHERE filter drops the all-NULL address row)
